metadata.fiscal_year_note: Nepal FY: Shrawan 1 – Ashadh end (BS calendar)

vat.note_en: Standard VAT 13%. Exports zero-rated. Registration mandatory if annual turnover exceeds NPR 50 lakhs.
vat.note_ne: Sadharan VAT 13% ho. Export zero-rated. Barsaiko turnover 50 lakh bhanda badi bhaye VAT registration anivarya.

ssf.note_en: SSF: employee 10% of basic, employer 11% of basic, plus 1% accident insurance (employer).
ssf.note_ne: SSF: karmachari 10% basic bata, employer 11% basic bata, 1% accident (employer).

tds.note_en: Common TDS: 1.5% goods/services contract, 10% urban rent, 15% professional, 15% interest, 5% dividend (individual).
tds.note_ne: Common TDS: karar 1.5%, sahari bhada 10%, professional 15%, byaj 15%, dividend 5% (byakti).

items.text_en: VAT registration is mandatory when annual taxable turnover exceeds NPR 50 lakhs (5 million). Standard rate is 13%. Exports are zero-rated; exempt supplies have no output VAT but input credit rules differ.
items.text_ne: Barsaiko taxable turnover 50 lakh bhanda badi bhaye VAT registration anivarya. Sadharan dar 13%. Export zero-rated; exempt ma output VAT chaina tara input credit ko niyam farak.

items.text_en: Zero-rated (exports): 0% VAT on sale but input VAT generally recoverable. Exempt supplies: no VAT charged, and input VAT on related purchases is usually not creditable.
items.text_ne: Zero-rated (export): bikri ma 0% VAT tara input VAT praya recover garna milchha. Exempt: VAT charge hudaina, ra sambandhit kharid ko input VAT praya credit hudaina.

items.text_en: TDS on rent in urban areas is generally 10% withheld at source. Record: Dr Rent Expense (gross), Cr TDS Payable, Cr Cash (net paid).
items.text_ne: Sahari area ma bhada ma praya 10% TDS katauncha. Entry: Dr Rent Expense (gross), Cr TDS Payable, Cr Cash (net).

items.text_en: Goods/supply contracts: often 1.5% TDS. Professional/consultancy services: commonly 15% TDS. Always check contract type and IRD section for exact rate.
items.text_ne: Maal/karar: praya 1.5% TDS. Professional/consultancy: praya 15% TDS. Karar prakar ra IRD section herera exact rate nikaalnu.

items.text_en: General corporate income tax rate in Nepal is 25% on taxable profit. Cooperatives may qualify for 5%. SEZ entities may get concessional rates.
items.text_ne: Nepal ma sadharan company ko income tax 25% taxable profit ma. Sahakari sanstha 5% hun sakchha. SEZ ma concession rate hun sakchha.

items.text_en: Primary agricultural income by individuals is generally exempt from income tax in Nepal, subject to IRD conditions and commercialisation thresholds.
items.text_ne: Byakti ko prathamik krishi aamdani Nepal ma sadharan exempt, tara IRD sharta ra commercial threshold hernu parchha.

items.text_en: NAS 1 covers overall presentation: complete set (SOFP, P/L, equity changes, cash flows, notes), going concern, accrual basis, and minimum line items for Nepal entities.
items.text_ne: NAS 1 le kul prastuti sametcha: balance sheet, P/L, equity changes, cash flow, notes; going concern, accrual basis, ra minimum line items.

items.text_en: NAS 2: inventories measured at lower of cost and NRV. Cost formulas: FIFO or weighted average (LIFO not permitted under NAS). Write-down to NRV when damaged/obsolete.
items.text_ne: NAS 2: inventory cost wa NRV jun kam ho. FIFO wa weighted average; LIFO NAS ma allowed chaina. Bigreko/obsolete bhaye NRV samma write-down.

items.text_en: NAS 16: recognise PPE at cost, depreciate over useful life (SLM or WDV common in Nepal). Major inspection/replacement may be capitalised; repairs are expense.
items.text_ne: NAS 16: PPE cost ma recognise, useful life ma depreciation (SLM/WDV common). Thulo repair capitalise; sadharan repair expense.

items.text_en: NAS 19 covers short-term, post-employment (gratuity, pension), and termination benefits. Nepal: gratuity provision + SSF contributions are common — accrue when obligation arises.
items.text_ne: NAS 19 le short-term, post-employment (gratuity), termination benefits cover garchha. Nepal ma gratuity provision ra SSF accrue garnu parchha.

items.text_en: Provision = liability of uncertain timing/amount when present obligation from past event exists. Contingent liability: disclose, do not recognise unless remote. Accrual records known amount when obligation is certain.
items.text_ne: Provision = uncertain timing/amount ko liability jaba present obligation chha. Contingent: disclose, recognise nagarnu (remote bhaye). Accrual = pakka obligation aayeko bela record.

items.text_en: NFRS 15: identify contract → performance obligations → transaction price → allocate → recognise when (or as) obligation satisfied. Credit sales still recognise revenue when control transfers, not when cash received.
items.text_ne: NFRS 15: contract → performance obligation → price → allocate → control transfer huda revenue recognise. Udhaar bikri ma pani control gayo bhane revenue, cash aayeko bela matra hoina.

items.text_en: NFRS 16: lessees recognise right-of-use asset and lease liability for most leases. Depreciate ROU asset; unwind lease liability as interest. Short-term/low-value leases may be expensed.
items.text_ne: NFRS 16: lessee le ROU asset ra lease liability recognise garchha. ROU depreciation; liability unwinding interest. Short-term/low-value lease expense ma jaana sakchha.

items.text_en: When you sell on credit: Dr Sundry Debtors, Cr Sales. Party owes you (asset). Payment later: Dr Cash/Bank, Cr Debtors.
items.text_ne: Udhaar ma becda: Dr Debtors, Cr Sales. Party le tirna baki (asset). Paisa aayepachi: Dr Cash, Cr Debtors.
examples_ne: Gita lai saman becheko credit ma

items.text_en: Customer pays outstanding: Dr Cash/Bank, Cr Sundry Debtors. 'Ram le tiryo' or 'Sita bata aayo' = payment received.
items.text_ne: Customer le tiryo: Dr Cash/Bank, Cr Debtors. 'Ram le tiryo' wa 'Sita bata aayo' = payment received.

items.text_en: Write-off (confirmed irrecoverable): Dr Bad Debts Expense, Cr Debtors. Provision (conservative estimate): Dr Bad Debts Expense, Cr Provision for Bad Debts. Recovery later: Dr Cash, Cr Bad Debts Recovered (income).
items.text_ne: Write-off: Dr Bad Debts Expense, Cr Debtors. Provision: Dr Bad Debts Expense, Cr Provision. Recovery: Dr Cash, Cr Bad Debts Recovered (income).

items.text_en: Accrual: Dr Salary Expense, Cr Salary Payable. Payment: Dr Salary Payable, Cr Bank. SSF employee 10% deducted; employer 11% additional expense. Example net pay = gross − 10% SSF employee.
items.text_ne: Accrual: Dr Salary Expense, Cr Salary Payable. Payment: Dr Salary Payable, Cr Bank. SSF employee 10% katauchha; employer 11% extra expense.

items.text_en: Credit/cash sale with VAT 13%: Dr Debtor/Cash (gross), Cr Sales (net), Cr Output VAT Payable. Net VAT payable = Output VAT − Input VAT, remitted to IRD monthly.
items.text_ne: VAT 13% bikri: Dr Debtor/Cash (gross), Cr Sales (net), Cr Output VAT. Net VAT = Output − Input, mahina ma IRD lai.

items.text_en: Cash in Hand and Bank are assets (debit balance). Increase on receipt; decrease on payment. Petty cash/imprest is also an asset, not expense.
items.text_ne: Cash ra Bank asset ho (debit balance). Aayo bhane badhchha; tiryo bhane ghanchha. Petty cash pani asset, expense hoina.

items.text_en: Input VAT on purchases is generally an asset (recoverable credit) until set off against output VAT. Not a regular expense if creditable.
items.text_ne: Kharid ko Input VAT sadharan asset ho (recoverable) jaba credit milchha. Regular expense hoina.

items.text_en: Asset = present economic resource controlled by the entity from past events (IFRS Para 4.3). In khata: Cash, Debtors, Stock, Fixed Assets, Prepaid, Input VAT.
items.text_ne: Sampatti = entity le control ma liyeko present economic resource. Khata ma: Cash, Debtors, Stock, Fixed Assets, Prepaid, Input VAT.

items.text_en: Udhaar can mean credit sale (they owe you = receivable) OR credit purchase (you owe them = payable). Context: 'X lai udhaar diye' = credit sale; 'X bata udhaar ma kineko' = credit purchase.
items.text_ne: Udhaar = udhaar bikri (receivable) wa udhaar kharid (payable). 'X lai udhaar diye' = bikri; 'X bata udhaar ma kineko' = kharid.

items.text_en: Accrual: record expense/income when bill/right is certain (e.g. unpaid electricity bill). Provision: estimate liability for uncertain amount (e.g. doubtful debts per NAS 37).
items.text_ne: Accrual: bill/rights pakka bhayepachi record. Provision: uncertain amount ko andaaja (jastai bad debt provision, NAS 37).

items.text_en: VAT-registered businesses file monthly VAT returns to IRD (typically by end of next month). Maintain tax invoices with mandatory fields: seller/buyer PAN, amount, VAT, invoice date/number.
items.text_ne: VAT registered business le mahina ko VAT return IRD lai file garchha (praya aglo mahina samm). Tax invoice ma PAN, rakam, VAT, date/number anivarya.

items.text_en: Company Act 2063 requires companies to maintain books of accounts for at least 7 years. IRD audits may request vouchers, ledgers, and tax records for this period.
items.text_ne: Company Act 2063 anusar lekha kitab kamti ma 7 barsa rakhnuparchha. IRD audit le yo avadhi ko voucher/ledger magna sakchha.

items.text_en: Eligible employees under Labour Act 2074 may be entitled to annual bonus (often referenced as ~8.33% of annual salary depending on profit/eligibility). Record as expense when obligation arises.
items.text_ne: Labour Act 2074 anusar eligible karmachari lai barsaiko bonus (praya ~8.33% jasto reference) hun sakchha. Obligation bhaepachi expense record.
